categories.label: AI & Cloud Infrastructure Investment
categories.description: Global data center buildouts, multi-billion-dollar capex commitments, energy procurement deals (nuclear, renewables), cloud capacity expansion, and large-scale capital deployment for AI infrastructure across regions.

categories.label: OpenAI Partnership & Governance
categories.description: Microsoft's evolving relationship with OpenAI including funding rounds, board dynamics, the Altman firing and reinstatement saga, structural changes to OpenAI's corporate form, talent flows between the two organizations, and competitive dynamics with OpenAI's enterprise ambitions.

categories.label: Antitrust, Regulatory & Legal
categories.description: EU antitrust charges (Teams bundling), cloud licensing complaints (CISPE, Google), FTC/CMA probes of the OpenAI investment, UK Inflection AI hiring scrutiny, data privacy fines, and other regulatory or legal actions worldwide.

categories.label: Cybersecurity & Service Reliability
categories.description: Russian state-sponsored hacks (Midnight Blizzard), the global CrowdStrike-linked IT outage, Azure and Microsoft 365 service disruptions, government security review criticism, congressional testimony on security lapses, and cybersecurity product launches.

categories.label: Strategic Partnerships & AI Products
categories.description: Enterprise partnerships (Vodafone, Siemens, Palantir, BlackRock, Sainsbury's, Cognizant), Copilot product launches, AI PC hardware, autonomous agent development, Azure Marketplace integrations, and go-to-market alliances.

categories.label: Gaming & Entertainment Strategy
categories.description: Xbox multi-platform strategy, Activision Blizzard integration and layoffs, Call of Duty subscription model shift, next-generation console planning, gaming workforce restructuring, and competitive positioning against Sony and Nintendo.

categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue and EPS figures versus estimates, cloud revenue guidance revisions, dividend declarations, the $60 billion share buyback authorization, and securities litigation tied to financial disclosures.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad tech sector rallies and selloffs, Nasdaq corrections, Fed rate decision impacts, AI speculation waves, Magnificent Seven narratives, and macro-driven market roundups mentioning Microsoft.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, rating reaffirmations, and initiation of coverage from major brokerages including Wedbush, UBS, HSBC, BofA, and New Street Research.

dimensions.scale: 1=trivial operational detail with no measurable effect on revenue, margins, or strategic position, 10=directly and substantially reshaping a major revenue stream, cost structure, or total addressable market
1=outcome fully expected and already reflected in consensus expectations, 10=outcome completely unanticipated by analysts and investors with no prior signals
1=impact confined to the next few trading days with no lasting structural effect, 10=structural shift affecting competitive position or business model for years
1=neutral or purely factual tone with no positive or negative valence, 10=extremely strong positive or negative framing with urgent or emphatic language
1=pure opinion, speculation, or rehashed narrative with no supporting data, 10=dense with specific financial figures, contractual terms, or quantitative evidence throughout
1=ambiguous implications that could reasonably be interpreted as positive or negative, 10=unambiguously good or bad for equity value with no reasonable counter-interpretation
1=affects only this company's specific operations or products, 5=sector-wide implications across multiple peers, 10=macro or market-wide event affecting all equities
1=no effect on competitive positioning relative to peers, 10=major shift in competitive dynamics altering market share or strategic advantage
1=no regulatory, legal, or governmental dimension, 10=major regulatory action with potential to force structural business changes or impose large penalties
1=no leadership, governance, or strategic direction angle, 10=major leadership change, strategic pivot announcement, or CEO-level decision revealing new corporate direction
1=transient story fully resolved within a single news cycle, 10=persistent story generating follow-up coverage and remaining material for weeks or months
1=reinforces the prevailing investor thesis with no new framing, 10=fundamentally challenges or reframes the consensus view of the company's trajectory
1=rehash or incremental follow-up of a previously reported story with no new information, 10=first report introducing genuinely new facts or developments not previously available to the market

1=no financial results reported or results exactly match prior guidance and consensus estimates, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst expectations as described in the article

kept.materiality: Ranges from trivial Azure Marketplace listings and small carbon credit purchases to multi-billion-dollar infrastructure commitments, $100B Stargate plans, and quarterly earnings revealing cloud growth trajectory. Wide spread across the corpus. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.surprise: Altman's firing was a genuine shock; Russian hack escalation and the CrowdStrike-linked global outage were unforeseeable; contrast with routine scheduled earnings dates, predictable data center investments, and expected dividend declarations. Strong variation across event types. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.temporal_horizon: Service outages resolve in hours; analyst target raises are near-term; EU antitrust charges, nuclear power purchase agreements, and structural AI infrastructure buildouts have multi-year implications. Clear spread from transient to permanent. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.sentiment_strength: Neutral tone in marketplace availability announcements and routine dividend declarations versus strongly negative security breach disclosures and scathing government review reports, and strongly positive earnings beats with AI growth narratives. Meaningful tonal variation. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.information_density: Ranges from one-line flash headlines with no data to detailed earnings releases with specific EPS, revenue, margin figures, segment breakdowns, and forward guidance. Wide variation. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.directional_clarity: Earnings beats are unambiguously positive; security breaches are clearly negative; antitrust probes and OpenAI governance reshuffling have genuinely ambiguous implications depending on interpretation. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.scope: Company-specific events like Xbox layoffs and Teams unbundling contrast sharply with sector-wide AI capex trends and macro articles about Fed rate decisions and Nasdaq corrections. Full range represented. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.competitive_impact: Google filing EU complaints against Microsoft cloud practices, OpenAI pitching enterprise customers directly, and the Mistral AI partnership diversifying beyond OpenAI all carry competitive implications — versus routine partnership announcements with no competitive shift. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.regulatory_risk: Extremely prominent and varied: EU Teams bundling charges, CISPE settlement, UK Inflection probe, FTC inquiry into OpenAI investment, LinkedIn GDPR fine, congressional security testimony — contrasted with many articles carrying zero regulatory dimension. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.management_signal: Hiring DeepMind co-founder Suleyman to lead AI, the Altman firing/reinstatement saga, Microsoft leaving OpenAI's board, Brad Smith testifying before Congress, hiring GE's ex-CFO — versus routine product announcements with no leadership angle. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.expected_duration: The CrowdStrike outage dominated one day then faded; the Russian hacker intrusion persisted across months of follow-up disclosures; EU antitrust proceedings generated articles spanning the entire corpus window. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.narrative_shift: Most articles reinforce the dominant AI-leader thesis; the security review board report shifted narrative toward security-negligent framing; the cloud revenue forecast cut challenged the AI-monetization story; Altman firing temporarily reframed OpenAI as liability rather than asset. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.repeatedness: Many stories appear as 5-10 near-duplicate articles about the same event (OpenAI $6.6B round, Three Mile Island deal, CISPE settlement). First reports carry new information; subsequent coverage adds little. High variation between breaking reports and nth rehash. Confirmed by all 3 runs.
kept.financial_result_surprise: Three quarterly earnings cycles with varying degrees of beat/miss; cloud guidance revision was a specific negative surprise; the $60B buyback and dividend increase carried concrete financial content — while most non-earnings articles score at the floor. Important binary separator for earnings events. Confirmed by all 3 runs.

dropped.actionability: Dropped by all 3 runs. Overlaps heavily with materiality and information_density — articles with hard financial data and high materiality are inherently the most actionable. Edge cases where actionability diverges are better captured by temporal_horizon. Would create redundant scoring without unique signal.
dropped.controversy: Dropped by all 3 runs. Most MSFT articles reflect consensus-aligned views. Even antitrust and security stories generated limited investor disagreement — the debate was about magnitude of impact, not direction. Would cluster near 1-3 for the vast majority of articles. The meaningful signal is better captured by directional_clarity and regulatory_risk.
dropped.geopolitical_sensitivity: Dropped — appeared in only 1 of 3 runs (Run 2). While the G42 congressional scrutiny and China staff relocation stories carry geopolitical weight, these events are adequately captured by the combination of regulatory_risk, scope, and competitive_impact. Not robust enough across independent analyses to warrant a standalone dimension.

categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue and EPS figures, profit margins, guidance revisions, stock split, dividend announcements, credit rating upgrades, and any article reporting specific financial results versus expectations.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, Magnificent Seven commentary, sector rotation, macro indicators (Fed policy, recession fears, inflation data), index milestones, and articles mentioning NVDA primarily as part of a wider market or tech narrative.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, buy/sell ratings from brokerages, institutional positioning disclosures, and sell-side research notes on NVDA.

categories.label: AI Platform & Chip Launches
categories.description: New chip architecture announcements (Blackwell, Hopper), GTC and CES/Computex product reveals, NIM microservices, DGX systems, networking hardware, inference and training benchmarks, and major platform capability expansions.

categories.label: Regulatory, Legal & Geopolitical Risk
categories.description: DOJ antitrust investigations, French competition authority charges, US export control updates targeting China, securities fraud lawsuits and investigations, Supreme Court PSLRA case, copyright infringement claims, and country-specific chip sales restrictions.

categories.label: Enterprise & Industry Partnerships
categories.description: Strategic partnerships with hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Oracle), enterprise software integrations (SAP, Cisco, ServiceNow, Accenture), AI deployments in healthcare, autonomous vehicles, robotics, digital twins, telecom, and manufacturing, plus acquisitions like Run:ai.

categories.label: Gaming & Consumer Products
categories.description: GeForce NOW game additions and promotions, RTX GPU launches (40 SUPER series), gaming events (Gamescom), consumer-facing software updates, DLSS and RTX video technology, and streaming technology partnerships.

categories.label: Global Expansion & Sovereign AI
categories.description: Country-level AI infrastructure investments and sovereign supercomputer deployments in India, Japan, Vietnam, Indonesia, Denmark, Thailand, and other nations, including government partnerships, international AI summits, and sovereign AI cloud initiatives.

categories.label: Competitive Landscape & Moat
categories.description: Huawei and AMD chip challenges, CUDA software ecosystem lock-in debates, competitor product launches, custom chip unit creation, market share dynamics, and articles framing NVDA's competitive positioning relative to rivals.

dimensions.scale: 1=no direct impact on revenue, margins, or addressable market; 10=directly and substantially affects near-term revenue, margin structure, or total addressable market size
1=outcome or content fully expected and already priced into consensus; 10=outcome or revelation completely unexpected relative to prevailing assumptions
1=affects only the next few trading days with no lasting significance; 10=represents a structural shift in competitive position, market structure, or business model lasting years
1=neutral or purely informational tone with no positive or negative valence; 10=extremely strong positive or negative sentiment conveyed through language and framing
1=pure opinion, speculation, or rehashed narrative with no new data; 10=rich in hard data, specific figures, quantified metrics, or detailed technical specifications
1=ambiguous implications that could reasonably be interpreted as positive or negative; 10=unambiguously positive or negative with clear directional implication for the stock
1=affects only this company specifically; 5=sector-wide implications for semiconductors or AI; 10=macro or market-wide event affecting broad asset classes
1=no implications for competitive positioning; 10=fundamentally reshapes competitive landscape by creating or destroying a major competitive advantage
1=no regulatory, legal, or policy dimension; 10=major regulatory action, legal ruling, or policy change with direct consequences for operations or market access
1=transient story fully resolved or forgotten within a day; 10=persistent story that remains in investor consciousness and generates follow-up coverage for weeks or months
1=reinforces the prevailing investment thesis and consensus narrative; 10=fundamentally challenges or redirects the dominant narrative about the company
1=rehash or follow-up of previously reported information adding no new facts; 10=first report of genuinely new information not previously available to the market
1=background context or educational content with no immediate trading implications; 10=contains specific, time-sensitive information investors can act on immediately

1=no financial results reported or results exactly match prior guidance and analyst expectations; 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst consensus as described in the article

kept.materiality: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from GeForce NOW weekly game additions and blog posts with zero revenue impact to Blackwell platform launches defining next-generation data center revenue, quarterly earnings revealing billions in results, and DOJ antitrust subpoenas with direct business consequences.
kept.surprise: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from fully anticipated events like pre-announced GTC keynotes and scheduled weekly GeForce NOW posts to genuinely unexpected revelations such as DOJ subpoena reports, Blackwell production delay leaks, and the gap between Q2 FY25 earnings that beat estimates but disappointed elevated expectations.
kept.temporal_horizon: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from single-day game launches and weekly market recaps to Blackwell architecture announcements, export control policy shifts, and antitrust investigations with multi-year structural implications for competitive position and market access.
kept.sentiment_strength: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from neutral technical blog posts and informational product descriptions to strongly positive earnings celebrations with global market rallies and strongly negative antitrust probe and market selloff coverage using superlatives like 'soaring,' 'stunning,' and 'plunged.'
kept.information_density: Consensus 3/3. Varies from pure opinion columns and market-mood pieces with no specific data to earnings reports with exact revenue, EPS, and margin figures, analyst notes with precise price targets, and product launches with detailed technical specifications.
kept.directional_clarity: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from ambiguous articles debating whether AI valuations are stretched or justified and antitrust probes that could be minor or existential, to clearly positive massive earnings beats and clearly negative subpoena reports with unambiguous directional implications.
kept.scope: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from NVDA-specific events like the Run:ai acquisition and CEO pay raise to sector-wide semiconductor demand signals to broad macro events like recession fears, Fed rate decisions, and global equity selloffs where NVDA is one of many affected.
kept.competitive_impact: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from routine partnership announcements with no competitive implication to Huawei launching rival AI chips, AMD data center events challenging dominance, Silicon Valley efforts to break CUDA lock-in, France charging anticompetitive practices, and the custom chip unit targeting the ASIC market.
kept.regulatory_risk: Consensus 3/3. Most product and partnership articles have zero regulatory angle while DOJ antitrust probes, French competition charges, evolving US-China export controls, Supreme Court securities fraud case, and securities fraud investigation announcements score very high, providing sharp variation.
kept.expected_duration: Consensus 2/3 (kept by Runs 2 and 3, dropped by Run 1 as overlapping with temporal_horizon). Ranges from highly transient daily market roundups and single-game GeForce NOW additions forgotten within hours to persistent multi-month stories like the DOJ antitrust investigation, Blackwell production timeline, and US-China export control evolution. Retained because it captures news-cycle persistence independently of structural business impact.
kept.narrative_shift: Consensus 3/3. Most articles reinforce the dominant AI-demand-is-insatiable thesis, but Blackwell production delays, DOJ antitrust escalation, dot-com bubble comparisons, semiconductor glut fears, earnings that beat but disappointed, and the custom chip unit represent genuine thesis challenges or evolutions.
kept.repeatedness: Consensus 3/3. Strong variation between first-report exclusives like the Reuters custom chip unit story, DOJ subpoena disclosure, and Blackwell delay leak versus heavily rehashed stories where multiple outlets repackage the same earnings figures, France antitrust developments, or DOJ probe details with no new facts.
kept.actionability: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from educational AI blog posts and corporate thought leadership providing background context to earnings releases, analyst target changes, antitrust subpoena reports, and chip delay disclosures that investors can and do act on within minutes.
kept.financial_result_surprise: Consensus 3/3. Most articles report no financial results, but the three earnings cycles show meaningful variation from the Q4 FY24 blowout with revenue up 265% that sparked global rallies, the Q1 FY25 beat triggering a stock split, and Q2 FY25 results that technically beat but fell short of elevated expectations.

dropped.controversy: Dropped by all 3 runs. NVDA coverage during this period was overwhelmingly consensus-directional rather than divisive. Even potentially contentious events like antitrust probes and bubble comparisons generated relatively one-sided investor reactions rather than genuine two-sided debate, making this dimension uninformative for differentiating articles.
dropped.management_signal: Dropped by 2 of 3 runs (kept only by Run 1). While Jensen Huang appears frequently, his presence is so pervasive across product launches, keynotes, and strategic commentary that most substantive articles carry some management signal. The dimension clusters around moderate values with insufficient discrimination, and the few exceptions are already captured by materiality and narrative_shift.
dropped.demand_signal_strength: Custom dimension proposed by Run 1 only. While AI demand sustainability is a dominant theme, it appeared in only 1 of 3 runs and is not robust under consensus rules. The concept is partially captured by the combination of materiality, narrative_shift, and competitive_impact.

categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, delivery and production numbers, revenue, margins, EPS, guidance revisions, energy storage deployment figures, and financial results relative to analyst expectations.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, sector rotation, macro data reactions, index movements, and general EV or tech industry sentiment that mentions the company as part of a wider narrative.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, buy/sell ratings, proxy advisor recommendations, and institutional investor positioning or recommendation changes.

categories.label: Autonomous Driving & Robotaxi
categories.description: Full Self-Driving software development, robotaxi strategy and unveil events, Cybercab design and reception, NHTSA Autopilot investigations, FSD regulatory approval efforts, and autonomous vehicle safety incidents.

categories.label: Musk Compensation & Governance
categories.description: The $56 billion pay package litigation and shareholder vote, Delaware court ruling, reincorporation to Texas, board independence concerns, proxy advisor recommendations, and demands for greater voting control.

categories.label: Pricing, Demand & Competition
categories.description: Vehicle price cuts and increases across global markets, demand signals, incentive programs, competitive dynamics with rivals, monthly regional sales data, EV price wars, and market share shifts.

categories.label: Recalls, Safety & Regulatory
categories.description: Vehicle recalls across all models, NHTSA safety investigations, steering and suspension probes, crash investigations, environmental compliance actions, and safety-related regulatory proceedings.

categories.label: Workforce & Operations
categories.description: Mass layoffs and restructuring, executive departures, factory shutdowns and disruptions, gigafactory expansion, labor strikes and union organizing, supply chain decisions, and supercharger team changes.

categories.label: Musk Political, Legal & Personal
categories.description: Political endorsements and campaign activity, SEC and DOJ enforcement actions, personal controversies, lawsuits involving other ventures, and personal conduct reporting that creates headline risk for the stock.

dimensions.scale: 1=trivial mention or tangential reference with no impact on revenue, margins, or addressable market, 10=event directly and substantially alters the company's revenue trajectory, margin structure, or total addressable market
1=outcome or content was fully anticipated and already reflected in consensus expectations, 10=outcome or revelation was completely unexpected and contradicts prevailing market expectations
1=impact confined to the next few trading days with no structural implications, 10=structural shift that reshapes the company's competitive position or business model for years
1=neutral or matter-of-fact tone with no evaluative language, 10=extremely strong positive or negative tone conveying urgency, euphoria, or alarm
1=pure opinion, speculation, or rehashed narrative with no new data points, 10=dense with specific hard numbers such as revenue figures, unit counts, margins, percentages, or regulatory findings
1=ambiguous implications that could reasonably be interpreted as either positive or negative for the stock, 10=unambiguously positive or negative with no room for competing interpretations
1=affects only this specific company with no sector or market implications, 10=macro or market-wide event affecting broad asset classes beyond any single company
1=no meaningful effect on the company's competitive position relative to rivals, 10=fundamentally reshapes the company's competitive standing in key markets
1=no regulatory, legal, or government enforcement angle whatsoever, 10=major regulatory action, investigation, or legal ruling with direct consequences for operations or liability
1=no leadership, strategic direction, or executive decision angle, 10=major strategic pivot, leadership change, or CEO commitment that redefines company direction
1=reinforces the prevailing investor thesis and existing market narrative about the company, 10=fundamentally challenges or reverses the dominant investment thesis
1=rehash or follow-up of a previously reported story adding no new information beyond prior coverage, 10=first report of genuinely new information not previously in the public domain
1=background context or color that provides no basis for immediate investment decisions, 10=concrete new information that investors can immediately incorporate into valuation or positioning

1=no financial results reported or reported figures exactly match prior guidance and consensus estimates, 10=reported financial figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst consensus as described in the article
1=consensus view with no meaningful disagreement among investors or stakeholders, 10=deeply polarizing event that divides investors, analysts, or public opinion on the company's direction

kept.materiality: Ranges from trivial mentions in market roundups and tangential personal anecdotes to events with massive direct financial impact such as cancellation of a major vehicle platform, quarterly earnings revealing margin compression or surprise beats, workforce reductions, and global pricing strategy changes. Appeared in all 3 runs.
kept.surprise: Clear variation from fully anticipated scheduled events meeting consensus to genuine shocks: major earnings beats after quarters of misses, abrupt product line cancellations that blindsided investors, factory arson attacks, and executive departures. Appeared in all 3 runs.
kept.temporal_horizon: Spans from single-day price adjustments and one-off recall notices to structural multi-year pivots like abandoning a product line in favor of a new business model, regulatory regime changes reshaping competitive dynamics for years, and ongoing compensation litigation sagas. Appeared in all 3 runs.
kept.sentiment_strength: Ranges from flat data releases and neutral regulatory filings to intensely charged coverage conveying alarm over safety fatalities, euphoria over earnings surprises, or combative executive rhetoric toward advertisers and critics. Appeared in all 3 runs.
kept.information_density: Wide spectrum from pure opinion columns and speculative analysis to articles packed with specific EPS figures, delivery counts, margin percentages, energy deployment numbers, and precise price change amounts across multiple markets. Appeared in all 3 runs.
kept.directional_clarity: Some events are unambiguously directional such as earnings beats or confirmed investigations, while others like tariff structures, executive control demands, or business model pivots have genuinely ambiguous implications that split investor interpretation. Appeared in all 3 runs.
kept.scope: Ranges from company-only events like specific model recalls and internal layoff notices through sector-wide EV dynamics like price wars and tariff regimes to macro-level stories about monetary policy and broad index rotation. Appeared in 2 of 3 runs (kept in Runs 1 and 2; Run 3 dropped it as lacking mid-range variation, but the other two runs demonstrated sufficient spread).
kept.competitive_impact: Strong variation: rival companies overtaking in global sales, competitor bankruptcies removing challengers, tariff structures reshaping import economics, and partnership announcements in adjacent markets, versus routine operational updates with no competitive dimension. Appeared in 2 of 3 runs (kept in Runs 2 and 3; Run 1 dropped it as capturable by materiality and scope, but the other two runs showed it captures distinct competitive-positioning signal).
kept.regulatory_risk: Massive variation: from articles with zero regulatory content to multiple active safety investigations following fatalities, securities enforcement actions against executives, fraud examinations of product claims, and tariff determinations affecting import costs. Appeared in all 3 runs.
kept.management_signal: Ranges from routine operational updates to extremely high-signal events: executive demands for greater voting control before advancing key initiatives, senior leadership departures, entire team eliminations, and strategic pivots that redefine the company's direction. Appeared in all 3 runs.
kept.narrative_shift: Most routine news reinforces existing narratives, but product line cancellations in favor of new business models, earnings turnarounds after quarters of deterioration, and executive political entanglements each challenged or reversed the prevailing investment thesis. Appeared in all 3 runs.
kept.repeatedness: Very high variation due to corpus structure: many articles are first-break reports of genuinely new events, while substantial portions are explicit rehashes across outlets reporting identical figures, boilerplate legal notices, or Nth articles on the same ongoing saga. Appeared in all 3 runs.
kept.actionability: Ranges from background color pieces and historical industry context to immediately actionable information: revised quarterly guidance, confirmed price changes effective on specific dates, delivery numbers versus consensus, and regulatory recall announcements with specific vehicle counts. Appeared in 2 of 3 runs (kept in Runs 1 and 2; Run 3 dropped it as lacking discriminating power, but the other two runs showed sufficient variation between background context and hard actionable data).
kept.financial_result_surprise: Highly relevant given multiple quarterly reporting cycles in the corpus spanning the full range: earnings misses with margin compression, delivery misses versus consensus, modest beats against lowered expectations, and dramatic upside surprises with analysts expressing genuine shock. Most non-earnings articles score at the floor, providing clean separation. Appeared in all 3 runs.
kept.controversy: Ranges from consensus-view delivery reports to deeply divisive events where major institutional investors and retail shareholders hold sharply opposing views, executive actions polarize the investor base, and product safety debates divide analysts. Appeared in 2 of 3 runs (kept in Runs 1 and 3; Run 2 dropped it as capturable by low directional_clarity plus high management_signal, but the other two runs showed controversy captures a distinct polarization signal beyond mere ambiguity).

dropped.expected_duration: Dropped — appeared in only 1 of 3 runs (kept only in Run 3). Both Run 1 and Run 2 independently dropped it as too correlated with temporal_horizon: events with long-term structural impact also tend to persist in news cycles, and short-horizon events fade quickly, producing near-identical scores across articles.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst price target changes, upgrades, downgrades, and rating reiterations from firms like Bank of America, UBS, Jefferies, and Wedbush, plus institutional positioning and valuation commentary.

dimensions.scale: 1=no connection to the company's revenue, margins, or addressable market (e.g., lifestyle puff piece or tangential mention), 10=direct and large-scale impact on a major business segment's financials or strategic direction
1=outcome or content fully expected and already priced into consensus expectations, 10=completely unexpected revelation or result that contradicts prevailing market assumptions
1=impact limited to the next few days with no structural consequence, 10=structural shift affecting the company's competitive position or business model for years
1=neutral or purely informational tone with no positive or negative valence, 10=extremely strong positive or negative tone conveying major success or serious threat
1=pure opinion, speculation, or promotional content with no verifiable data points, 10=rich in hard data including specific financial figures, contractual terms, or quantified outcomes
1=ambiguous implications that could reasonably be interpreted as positive or negative depending on framing, 10=unambiguously good or bad for the company with no reasonable alternative interpretation
1=affects only this company with no read-through to peers or sector, 10=macro or market-wide event where the company is one of many affected parties
1=no meaningful effect on competitive positioning relative to rivals, 10=fundamentally alters competitive dynamics in a major market the company operates in
1=no regulatory, legal, or governmental dimension whatsoever, 10=major regulatory action or legal ruling with potential to force structural business changes or impose significant financial penalties
1=no leadership, strategic, or organizational dimension, 10=major leadership change, strategic pivot announcement, or board-level governance decision signaling new direction
1=reinforces the existing consensus investment thesis about the company, 10=fundamentally challenges or changes the dominant narrative about the company's trajectory
1=rehash or follow-up of previously reported information with no new substance, 10=first report of genuinely new information not previously available to the market

1=no financial results reported or results exactly match prior guidance and analyst consensus, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst expectations as described in the article

kept.materiality: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from how-to guides and promotional deal listicles with zero financial relevance to multi-billion-dollar AWS infrastructure commitments and quarterly earnings revealing segment-level performance shifts.
kept.surprise: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from pre-announced Prime Day dates and scheduled earnings calls to unexpected events like the five-day RTO mandate, AWS CEO departure, iRobot deal collapse, and nuclear energy pivots.
kept.temporal_horizon: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from single-day Prime Day deal roundups and weekly entertainment premieres to 15-year data center investment commitments and nuclear power supply agreements reshaping the company's cost structure for a decade.
kept.sentiment_strength: Consensus 3/3. Spans from neutral product guides and device how-to articles to strongly positive earnings beats with shares jumping 6-8%, and strongly negative coverage of strikes, antitrust rulings, and surveillance fines.
kept.information_density: Consensus 3/3. Enormous variation from pure marketing copy about Prime membership benefits to earnings releases with specific revenue, EPS, and segment growth figures, and AWS deals citing exact dollar commitments.
kept.directional_clarity: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from ambiguous articles like Anthropic investment scrutiny (unclear if threat or validation) and Alexa AI revamp (opportunity or admission of failure?) to unambiguous earnings beats or clear regulatory fines.
kept.scope: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from company-specific operational updates (new fulfillment center, device launch) to sector-wide articles about tech valuations and Magnificent Seven narratives to macro events like the August recession scare affecting all equities.
kept.competitive_impact: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from routine operational articles to the NBA broadcasting rights deal shifting sports streaming competition, the low-cost Temu-rival storefront challenging discount e-commerce, and nuclear energy deals in the cloud infrastructure arms race.
kept.regulatory_risk: Consensus 3/3. Exceptionally varied—from articles with zero regulatory content to FTC antitrust litigation surviving dismissal, EU blocking the iRobot acquisition, Italy seizing 121M euros, France's 32M euro surveillance fine, and CPSC ruling Amazon liable for third-party products.
kept.management_signal: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from no leadership dimension in routine product articles to high-signal events like the AWS CEO transition (Selipsky to Garman), Andy Jassy's strategic shareholder letter, the five-day RTO mandate, and Andrew Ng's board appointment.
kept.narrative_shift: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from thesis-reinforcing articles about AWS growth and Prime ecosystem expansion to potentially thesis-altering events like pursuing nuclear energy for AI, crossing $2T valuation, Dow Jones inclusion, and the Alexa subscription model pivot.
kept.repeatedness: Consensus 3/3. Extreme variation—dozens of near-identical Prime Day deal roundups and streaming how-to-watch articles recycled across outlets versus first-report breaking news like the RTO mandate, AWS CEO change, and iRobot deal collapse.
kept.financial_result_surprise: Consensus 3/3. Most articles report no financial results, but quarterly earnings articles show clear variation: Q4 2023 and Q1 2024 showed strong beats driving share surges, while Q2 2024 showed slowing online sales growth causing an 8% share decline.

dropped.actionability: Kept in 1/3 runs (Run 3), explicitly dropped in 2/3 (Runs 1 and 2). Both dropping runs noted it is largely a composite of materiality, information density, and directional clarity rather than an independent signal—articles scoring high on those three are inherently the actionable ones.
dropped.controversy: Kept in 1/3 runs (Run 1), explicitly dropped in 2/3 (Runs 2 and 3). Both dropping runs noted the vast majority of AMZN articles cluster at low controversy, and the few divisive stories (RTO mandate, union battles) are better captured through the interaction of sentiment_strength and directional_clarity.
dropped.expected_duration: Dropped by all 3 runs. Universally judged redundant with temporal_horizon—the distinction between how long an impact lasts and how long a story stays in the news cycle does not produce meaningfully different rankings across AMZN articles.

categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue/EPS figures, profit margins, guidance, dividends, capex forecasts, stock buybacks, and any article reporting specific financial results versus expectations.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, sector rotation, macro data mentions, index movements, and multi-stock previews where META is mentioned alongside peers rather than as the primary subject.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, buy/sell ratings, and institutional positioning commentary on META specifically.

categories.label: AI Strategy & Infrastructure
categories.description: Development and release of AI models (Llama, Movie Gen, Meta AI assistant), AI infrastructure investments, custom chips, data center buildouts, energy deals, open-source AI strategy, and AI-powered product features.

categories.label: Regulatory, Antitrust & Compliance
categories.description: EU DMA/DSA proceedings, antitrust fines, FTC actions, competition probes, data privacy rulings, GDPR enforcement, government investigations, and regulatory actions targeting Meta's business practices across jurisdictions.

categories.label: Child Safety & Content Moderation
categories.description: Lawsuits and legislation targeting youth social media use, teen account protections, sextortion enforcement, content moderation policies, Senate hearings on child exploitation, and platform safety features for minors.

categories.label: Hardware, AR/VR & Metaverse
categories.description: Quest headsets, Orion AR glasses, Reality Labs initiatives, EssilorLuxottica smart eyewear partnership, mixed reality ecosystem, and hardware manufacturing strategy.

categories.label: Platform & Product Updates
categories.description: Feature launches across Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Threads, and Messenger including creator tools, business integrations, advertising capabilities, music deals, service outages, and product updates.

categories.label: Geopolitical & Government Relations
categories.description: Country-specific bans and access restrictions (Turkey, Russia, Brazil), government content removal requests, election integrity measures, foreign interference takedowns, political content decisions, and international policy disputes.

dimensions.scale: 1=routine operational update, minor product tweak, or corporate filing with no revenue/margin implications, 10=event directly and substantially impacts revenue streams, cost structure, or total addressable market
1=outcome fully expected or information already widely known, 10=completely unexpected revelation that contradicts prior consensus or assumptions
1=transient event affecting only the next few days, 10=structural shift with implications lasting years
1=neutral or balanced tone with no clear positive/negative lean, 10=extremely strong positive or negative tone throughout the article
1=pure opinion, speculation, or vague commentary with no specific data, 10=rich in hard data, specific figures, named sources, and quantified outcomes
1=ambiguous implications that could reasonably be interpreted as positive or negative, 10=unambiguously good or bad for the company with no reasonable counter-interpretation
1=affects only this company specifically, 5=sector-wide implications, 10=broad macro or market-wide event
1=no regulatory, legal, or compliance dimension, 10=major legal ruling, regulatory fine, or compliance mandate with direct financial or operational consequences
1=no effect on competitive positioning, 10=major shift in competitive dynamics through partnerships, market entry, or rival disruption
1=no leadership involvement or strategic implication, 10=major CEO/executive-level strategic announcement, organizational restructuring, or leadership change signaling new direction
1=reinforces the prevailing investment thesis and existing narrative about the company, 10=fundamentally challenges or redirects the market's understanding of the company's trajectory
1=rehash or follow-up of previously reported information with no new facts, 10=first report of genuinely new information not previously in the public domain
1=no financial results reported or results exactly match expectations, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst expectations as described in the article
1=transient story that resolves or fades within a day, 10=persistent story that remains relevant and develops over weeks or months

kept.materiality: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from Form 4 filings and minor feature announcements to $1.4B settlements and quarterly earnings with major capex guidance — wide variation across the corpus.
kept.surprise: Consensus 3/3. Spans from scheduled product launches and routine regulatory actions to the first-ever dividend declaration, 16% share price plunge on unexpected capex guidance, and $1.4B Texas settlement — strong variation in how unexpected outcomes were.
kept.temporal_horizon: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from one-day service outages to structural shifts like the EU DMA compliance model, open-source AI strategy, and Orion AR glasses prototype revealing a decade-long hardware strategy.
kept.sentiment_strength: Consensus 3/3. Varies from neutral corporate blog posts and Form 4 filings to strongly negative articles about child exploitation hearings and strongly positive articles about tripled earnings and dividend initiation.
kept.information_density: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from one-line headline fragments and opinion pieces to detailed earnings transcripts with specific revenue, EPS, margin figures, and analyst price targets.
kept.directional_clarity: Consensus 3/3. Some articles are clearly positive (earnings beat, dividend) or negative (antitrust fines, settlement), while others like AI spending increases are genuinely ambiguous across time horizons.
kept.scope: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from META-only events like Threads feature updates to sector-wide Big Tech regulatory crackdowns and broad macro articles about market selloffs.
kept.regulatory_risk: Consensus 3/3. Highly variable — from zero regulatory content in product announcements to major EU DMA charges, $1.4B Texas settlement, FTC privacy actions, and multiple country-level investigations.
kept.competitive_impact: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from no competitive angle in routine updates to major competitive moves like open-sourcing Llama 3, EssilorLuxottica partnership, and Movie Gen rivaling OpenAI's Sora.
kept.management_signal: Consensus 2/3 (kept runs 1, 3; run 2 dropped in favor of custom strategic_signal). Ranges from no leadership angle in routine news to Zuckerberg's Senate apology, AI team reorganization, open-source AI vision letters, and Reality Labs restructuring.
kept.narrative_shift: Consensus 3/3. Most articles reinforce existing narratives, but some shift the thesis — first dividend signals capital return era, Q1 capex guidance sparks AI spending fears, Orion prototype reframes hardware ambitions.
kept.repeatedness: Consensus 3/3. Clear variation between first-break stories (dividend announcement, Orion reveal, Texas settlement) and repeated multi-outlet coverage of the same events or follow-up articles days later.
kept.financial_result_surprise: Consensus 3/3. Most articles contain no financial results, but earnings articles show clear variation — Q4 2023 EPS tripled expectations while Q1 2024 guidance disappointed and shares plunged 16%.
kept.expected_duration: Consensus 2/3 (kept runs 1, 3; run 2 dropped as redundant with temporal_horizon). Retained because temporal_horizon captures how far forward the impact reaches while expected_duration captures how long the news story itself persists in coverage — a regulatory ruling may have a years-long horizon but fade from headlines in days.

dropped.actionability: Dropped by all 3 runs. Clusters at extremes — earnings and analyst actions are immediately actionable while regulatory and product articles are not — largely duplicates materiality without adding discriminating power.
dropped.controversy: Dropped by all 3 runs. Most META articles reflect consensus views rather than divisive investor debates. The few controversial items overlap heavily with directional_clarity and would score uniformly low for investor-relevant controversy.
dropped.strategic_signal: Custom dimension from run 2 only (1/1). The underlying concept overlaps substantially with management_signal which was kept by 2/3 runs. Not robust enough for consensus.

categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue and EPS figures, profit margins, guidance revisions, share buyback authorizations, dividend changes, and any article reporting specific financial results versus expectations.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, Magnificent Seven narratives, tech sector rotations, macro-driven selloffs or rallies, and general market-wide news that mentions AAPL as part of a wider basket.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, buy/sell/hold ratings, institutional positioning calls, and conviction list additions or removals from brokerages covering AAPL.

categories.label: Product Launches & Hardware
categories.description: iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, AirPods, and Vision Pro announcements, launches, spec reveals, teardowns, new chip introductions, associated software OS releases, and early demand or sales data for new devices.

categories.label: AI Strategy & Innovation
categories.description: Apple Intelligence development and rollout, OpenAI and Google Gemini partnerships, WWDC AI announcements, on-device AI chip development, competitive positioning in the generative AI race, and major strategic pivots including the Apple Car cancellation and redirection to AI.

categories.label: China Market & Competitive Dynamics
categories.description: iPhone sales trends in China, Huawei market share gains and smartphone competition, Tim Cook visits to Beijing, competitive threats from domestic brands, Samsung or other rivals overtaking Apple in global or regional rankings, and regulatory dynamics specific to China.

categories.label: Regulatory, Antitrust & Legal
categories.description: EU Digital Markets Act enforcement and fines, DOJ antitrust lawsuit, Epic Games disputes, Masimo patent litigation, Apple Watch import bans, App Store fee litigation, India antitrust probes, Japan app store regulation, and antitrust investigations in other jurisdictions.

categories.label: Supply Chain & Manufacturing
categories.description: India and Vietnam manufacturing expansion, Foxconn and Tata facility operations and quarterly results, supply disruptions from fires or weather, production volume targets, supplier labor practices, and geographic diversification of assembly operations away from China.

categories.label: Corporate Strategy & Operations
categories.description: Major strategic pivots, workforce layoffs and restructuring, acquisitions, labor union activity, leadership departures, environmental or CSR initiatives, services and ecosystem strategy shifts, and content or partnership deals.

dimensions.scale: 1=trivial noise with no plausible impact on revenue, margins, or addressable market, 10=direct and large-scale impact on a major revenue segment, cost structure, or total addressable market
1=outcome or content fully expected and already priced into consensus expectations, 10=outcome or revealed information completely unexpected by the market and analyst community
1=affects the company's outlook for days to weeks only, 10=structural shift that reshapes the company's trajectory for years
1=neutral or matter-of-fact tone with no positive or negative valence, 10=extremely strong positive or negative sentiment conveyed through language and framing
1=pure opinion, speculation, or rehashed narrative with no concrete data, 10=rich in hard data including specific financial figures, unit volumes, market share percentages, or verifiable metrics
1=ambiguous implications that could reasonably be interpreted as positive or negative for the stock, 10=unambiguously and clearly positive or negative with no room for alternative interpretation
1=affects only this company with no read-through to peers, 5=sector-wide implications, 10=macro or market-wide event affecting all equities
1=no effect on competitive positioning or market share dynamics, 10=major shift in competitive landscape that directly threatens or strengthens market position against key rivals
1=no regulatory, legal, or governmental dimension, 10=major regulatory action with potential to force structural business model changes or impose large financial penalties
1=no leadership, strategic, or organizational dimension, 10=major strategic pivot, C-suite action, or organizational restructuring that signals a fundamental change in company direction
1=transient story that resolves or becomes irrelevant within a day or two, 10=persistent story that remains in investor focus for weeks or months and generates follow-up coverage
1=reinforces the prevailing investor thesis and existing consensus narrative, 10=fundamentally challenges or changes the dominant investment thesis for the company
1=rehash, follow-up, or incremental update on a well-known and previously reported story, 10=first report of genuinely new information not previously available to the market

1=no financial results reported or results exactly in line with prior guidance and analyst expectations, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst consensus as described in the article

kept.materiality: Consensus across all 3 runs. Ranges from trivial product color additions and Apple Arcade game launches to DOJ antitrust lawsuits threatening the core business model and $110 billion buyback announcements directly affecting shareholder value. Wide variation across the corpus makes this highly discriminating.
kept.surprise: Consensus across all 3 runs. Scheduled product launches with expected specs score low, while the Apple Car cancellation with pivot to AI, DOJ antitrust lawsuit filing timing, OpenAI partnership announcement, and 40% iPhone shipment surge in China after months of decline represent high surprise outcomes that moved the stock.
kept.temporal_horizon: Consensus across all 3 runs. Ranges from transient iOS bugs and one-day service outages to multi-year structural shifts like the DOJ antitrust litigation, EU DMA compliance requirements, India manufacturing build-out, and the strategic AI pivot reshaping the company's trajectory.
kept.sentiment_strength: Consensus across all 3 runs. Many articles are neutral product spec sheets or factual regulatory updates, while China sales plunge reports, the AI-driven record stock rally after WWDC, DOJ monopoly accusations, and analyst declarations of a growth renaissance carry extreme sentiment.
kept.information_density: Consensus across all 3 runs. Ranges from vague opinion pieces about foldable iPhone timelines and AI potential to earnings reports with specific EPS, revenue, and segment breakdowns, and IDC/Counterpoint data with precise shipment percentages and market share figures.
kept.directional_clarity: Consensus across all 3 runs. iPhone 16 demand generated contradictory signals across the corpus with simultaneous data showing China gains and weakness, versus unambiguously directional events like the Barclays downgrade, Apple Watch import ban, record $110 billion buyback authorization, and 20% iPhone 16 sales surge.
kept.scope: Consensus across all 3 runs. Apple-specific patent disputes and product color announcements affect only AAPL, while EU DMA enforcement affects all designated gatekeepers, Magnificent Seven narratives move the entire tech sector, and global recession-fear selloffs are macro-wide events.
kept.competitive_impact: Consensus across all 3 runs. Ranges from routine product updates with no competitive read-through to Huawei doubling shipments and overtaking Apple in China, Samsung reclaiming global smartphone leadership, Harmony OS threatening the Android-iOS duopoly, and EU forcing ecosystem openness.
kept.regulatory_risk: Consensus across all 3 runs. The corpus is dominated by regulatory news — DOJ antitrust suit, EU DMA charges, EU €500M+ fines, India antitrust findings, Japan app store law, Apple Watch import bans, multiple patent disputes — while many product and earnings articles have zero regulatory dimension, creating wide variation.
kept.management_signal: Consensus across all 3 runs. Ranges from no leadership angle in routine software updates to the Apple Car cancellation with workforce redirection to AI, 600+ employee layoffs after project cancellations, Tim Cook's repeated China/SE Asia visits signaling geographic priorities, and the departure of Apple's head of iPhone and Watch design.
kept.expected_duration: Consensus across all 3 runs. One-day service outages and iOS bug reports fade immediately, while the DOJ antitrust lawsuit, EU DMA compliance saga, Epic Games dispute, and India manufacturing expansion generated months of continuous follow-up coverage across the corpus.
kept.narrative_shift: Consensus across all 3 runs. Routine quarterly product refreshes reinforce the existing thesis, while the Apple Intelligence announcement at WWDC fundamentally reframed the investment narrative from hardware stagnation to AI-driven upgrade cycle, and the Apple Car cancellation reshaped views on capital allocation strategy.
kept.repeatedness: Consensus across all 3 runs. The corpus contains extensive duplication where the same iPhone 16 launch, DOJ lawsuit, or EU ruling is covered by dozens of outlets with decreasing novelty and no new information, contrasted with genuine first reports like the Bloomberg scoop on Apple Car cancellation or the DarwinAI acquisition.
kept.financial_result_surprise: Consensus across all 3 runs. Most non-earnings articles contain no financial results at all, while earnings articles show clear variation from in-line results with lukewarm guidance to Q1 FY24 EPS beating estimates while China sales disappointed, and Q2 showing the steepest iPhone decline since the pandemic alongside the record $110B buyback that exceeded expectations.

dropped.controversy: Dropped by all 3 runs. AAPL's investor base is overwhelmingly institutional with relatively aligned views. Regulatory and labor stories are more clearly negative than contested, product launches are generally consensus-positive, and even the AI strategy was quickly embraced. The dimension would cluster around low values for the vast majority of articles with insufficient variation to be informative.
dropped.actionability: Dropped by 2 of 3 runs (Runs 1 and 3). Most AAPL news is immediately actionable given the stock's extreme liquidity and institutional coverage, causing the dimension to cluster around moderate-to-high values. Run 3 additionally noted it correlates heavily with the combination of directional_clarity and information_density, making it redundant in the scoring framework.

categories.label: Materiality
categories.description: Earnings Beat/Miss, Product Recall, Major Contract Win/Loss, Bankruptcy Filing, Regulatory Penalty, M&A Completion. 0 = noise with no impact, 10 = direct large impact on revenue/margins/TAM.

categories.label: Surprise & Unexpectedness
categories.description: Sudden CEO Departure, Unannounced Acquisition, Regulatory Shock, Unexpected Dividend Cut, Flash Pre-announcement. 0 = fully expected outcome, 10 = completely unexpected outcome.

categories.label: Sentiment Strength
categories.description: Earnings Blowout, Catastrophic Loss, Transformative Partnership, Fraud Revelation, Analyst Consensus Reversal. 0 = neutral tone, 10 = extremely strong positive or negative signal.

categories.label: Directional Clarity
categories.description: Clear Guidance Raise/Cut, FDA Approval/Rejection, Definitive Merger Agreement, Regulatory Ruling, Contract Win/Loss. 0 = ambiguous/could go either way, 10 = unambiguously good or bad.

categories.label: Information Density
categories.description: Full Earnings Release, Detailed Guidance, SEC Filing with Data, Analyst Research Report, Verified Technical Data. 0 = pure opinion/speculation, 10 = hard data with specific numbers.

categories.label: Impact Timeline & Duration
categories.description: Score how long the price impact is likely to persist: 0-3 = fades within days (sentiment-only, speculative, one-time); 4-6 = resolves within weeks to months; 7-10 = permanently alters business trajectory (structural, confirmed, recurring).

categories.label: Narrative Shift
categories.description: CEO Scandal reversing trust, Earnings Miss breaking growth story, Turnaround Success, Business Model Pivot. 0 = reinforces existing narrative, 10 = fundamentally changes investment thesis.

categories.label: Scope & Breadth
categories.description: Full Company Guidance, CEO Departure, Capital Structure Change, Single Product Issue, Regional Sales Miss. 0 = only this company/product, 5 = sector-wide, 10 = macro/market-wide.

categories.label: Competitive Impact
categories.description: Competitor Bankruptcy, Patent Win/Loss, Market Share Data Release, Competitor Product Launch, Pricing War. 0 = no competitive effect, 10 = major competitive shift.

categories.label: Regulatory Risk
categories.description: Antitrust Investigation, FDA Rejection, SEC Investigation, Tax Ruling, License Revocation, Compliance Violation. 0 = no regulatory angle, 10 = major legal/regulatory event.

categories.label: Financial Result Surprise
categories.description: EPS Beat/Miss vs Consensus, Revenue vs Guidance, Margin Surprise, Cash Flow Miss, Financial Restatement. 0 = no financial results or results exactly match expectations, 10 = figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst expectations.

categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Earnings Release, Revenue Guidance, Profit Warning, Annual Report, Quarterly Filing, Financial Restatement. Score how central this news is to the company's reported financial performance.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Sector Rotation, Industry-wide Regulation, Commodity Price Move, Macro Data Release, Fed Decision, Peer Earnings. Score how much this news reflects broad market or sector-level dynamics.

categories.label: Analyst Consensus Signals
categories.description: Rating Upgrade/Downgrade, Price Target Revision, Analyst Initiation, Consensus Estimate Change, Analyst Day. Score how much this news reflects analyst community views on the stock.

categories.label: Catalyst Immediacy
categories.description: Trading Halt, Breaking Announcement, Flash Pre-announcement, Scheduled Earnings, Long-term Roadmap Reveal. 0 = background/long-term context only, 10 = immediate market-moving catalyst.

categories.label: Systemic & Contagion Risk
categories.description: Banking Sector Stress, Supply Chain Collapse, Industry-wide Regulatory Sweep, Peer Earnings Warning, Macro Shock. Score how much this news signals risk spreading beyond the single company.

categories.label: Management & Credibility
categories.description: CEO/CFO Appointment or Resignation, Insider Trading, Executive Misconduct, Board Restructuring, Governance Change. Score how much this news affects trust in and stability of leadership.

categories.label: Capital Allocation Signal
categories.description: Share Buyback Program, Dividend Initiation/Cut, Major CapEx Announcement, Debt Issuance, Equity Offering. Score how much this news reveals management's capital deployment priorities.

categories.label: Valuation & Growth Outlook
categories.description: Long-term Growth Rate Revision, Margin Expansion/Compression, TAM Update, Business Model Change, Multiple Re-rating. Score how much this news changes the long-term growth or valuation narrative.

categories.label: Sentiment Direction
categories.description: Score the net directional tone of the news: 0 = entirely negative/bearish, 5 = neutral or balanced with no clear lean, 10 = entirely positive/bullish. Score the article's net tone — a mixed article with more negative weight scores 3, not 5.

categories.label: Gemini & AI Product Development
categories.description: Launches, updates, and controversies around Google's proprietary AI models (Gemini, AlphaFold), custom AI chips (Trillium, Axion), and integration of generative AI into Search, Workspace, and other Google products.

categories.label: Search & Ad Tech Antitrust
categories.description: DOJ search monopoly ruling and breakup proposals, ad tech trial proceedings, Play Store antitrust orders (Epic Games), and EU ad abuse fines that threaten the structural integrity of Google's core revenue engine.

categories.label: Google Cloud & Enterprise AI
categories.description: Google Cloud revenue performance, enterprise AI partnership announcements (Accenture, Thales, Vodafone, CMA CGM, Bayer, Hitachi), Vertex AI platform deals, and Cloud's role as the primary diversification engine away from advertising.

categories.label: Data Center & Energy Infrastructure
categories.description: Multi-billion-dollar global data center buildouts (Malaysia, Thailand, UK, South Carolina, Indiana), nuclear power agreements (Kairos SMRs), clean energy procurement, geothermal deals, and submarine cable deployments that underpin AI and Cloud capacity.

categories.label: Waymo Autonomous Vehicles
categories.description: Waymo robotaxi service expansion (Los Angeles, San Francisco), NHTSA safety investigations, cyclist and pedestrian incidents, software recalls, and California regulatory reviews of driverless deployment permits.

categories.label: YouTube Platform & Content
categories.description: YouTube advertising revenue trends, content moderation enforcement (extremist channels, gambling ads, political content), creator and shopping features (Shopee partnership, context notes), and regional access restrictions (Russia throttling, Hong Kong court orders).

categories.label: User Data & Privacy Practices
categories.description: Chrome third-party cookie deprecation plans and UK CMA oversight, privacy lawsuit settlements and browsing data destruction orders, EU and Irish data protection investigations into AI training data, and children's online safety compliance obligations.

categories.label: Digital Platform Compliance
categories.description: Ongoing compliance with DMA and DSA mandates, Chrome third-party cookie policies, news publisher licensing disputes and fines (France, Canada), India app store restoration orders, Russia content fines, and global digital services tax exposure.

categories.label: AI Investments & Talent Acquisition
categories.description: Strategic AI investments (Anthropic partnership and UK antitrust scrutiny), acqui-hire deals (Character.AI cofounders), Gemini app team consolidation into DeepMind, AI talent competition in Europe, and trade secret theft cases involving former AI engineers.
